frontmatter.title: Chasing Chapters
frontmatter.description: A community-driven book discovery and reading tracker app with personalized recommendations, reading streaks, and social features for book enthusiasts.

chasing-chapters.bodyHtml:
Chasing Chapters

A community-driven book discovery platform that combines personal reading tracking with social discovery features. Users can log books, track reading progress, join book clubs, and receive personalized recommendations.

Key Features

  • Reading Tracker: Log books, track pages/chapters, maintain reading streaks
  • Smart Recommendations: ML-powered suggestions based on reading history and preferences
  • Book Clubs: Create or join reading groups with shared bookshelves and discussion forums
  • Social Feed: Share reviews, ratings, and reading milestones with followers
  • Advanced Search: Elasticsearch-powered full-text search across millions of books

Tech Stack

Python/Django backend with PostgreSQL as the primary database and Elasticsearch for full-text search capabilities. Redis serves as both a cache and message broker for Celery task queues.

Architecture

The application uses a service-oriented architecture within Django, with separate apps for books, users, social features, and recommendations. Celery handles async tasks like recommendation generation and email notifications.

frontmatter.title: Icarus Server Manager
frontmatter.description: A lightweight, self-hosted server management dashboard for monitoring system metrics, managing Docker containers, and orchestrating deployments via a clean web UI.

icarus-server-manager.bodyHtml:
Icarus Server Manager

A lightweight, self-hosted server management dashboard that brings together system monitoring, Docker container management, and deployment orchestration in a clean, accessible web interface.

Key Features

  • Real-time Monitoring: Live system metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network) with historical graphing
  • Docker Management: Start, stop, restart, and view logs for containers directly from the dashboard
  • Deployment Orchestration: One-click deployment from GitHub webhooks with rollback support
  • User Management: Role-based access control for team environments
  • Alert System: Configurable thresholds with email and webhook notifications

Tech Stack

Built with TypeScript across the stack — Node.js/Express backend, PostgreSQL for metrics storage, Redis for caching and real-time pub/sub, and Docker for containerized deployment.

Architecture

The application follows a modular monolith pattern, with clear separation between the API layer, service layer, and data access layer. WebSocket connections provide real-time metric streaming to the dashboard frontend.

frontmatter.title: Tubular Bexus OSW
frontmatter.description: An experimental real-time data pipeline framework for streaming ETL transformations, built with a custom actor model for fault-tolerant, low-latency data processing.

tubular-bexus-osw.bodyHtml:
Tubular Bexus OSW

An experimental real-time data pipeline framework that implements a custom actor model for streaming ETL transformations. Built for fault-tolerant, low-latency data processing at scale.

Key Features

  • Actor Model: Lightweight actors with message-passing for parallel data processing
  • Streaming ETL: Real-time transformation pipelines with exactly-once semantics
  • Schema Evolution: Arrow-based schema management with automatic migration
  • Fault Tolerance: Supervised actor trees with automatic recovery
  • gRPC API: High-performance bidirectional streaming for data ingestion and retrieval

Tech Stack

Written entirely in Rust, leveraging Tokio for async runtime, Apache Kafka for stream ingestion, Arrow for columnar data processing, and gRPC for inter-service communication.

Architecture

Each pipeline stage runs as a supervised actor within a Tokio task. Data flows through a directed acyclic graph of transformation stages, with backpressure handling at every level. Checkpointing to Kafka ensures exactly-once processing guarantees.
